objective: Compare and order three or more fractions from least to greatest or greatest to least using models, benchmarks, and reasoning

pretest.question: Which list is in order from least to greatest?
Which fraction is the greatest: 3/8, 6/8, or 2/8?

learn.instruction: To ORDER fractions, compare them two at a time using the strategies you know. Step 1: Find the smallest (or largest) fraction. Step 2: Compare the remaining fractions. Step 3: Write them in order. Example: Order 4/6, 1/6, 5/6 from least to greatest. All have denominator 6 → just order numerators: 1 < 4 < 5 → 1/6, 4/6, 5/6.
learn.question: Order from least to greatest: 7/8, 2/8, 4/8

learn.instruction: When fractions have the SAME NUMERATOR, remember: smaller denominator = bigger pieces = greater fraction. Example: Order 3/8, 3/4, 3/6 from greatest to least. Same numerator (3). Order denominators smallest to largest for greatest to least: 4, 6, 8 → 3/4, 3/6, 3/8.
learn.question: Order from greatest to least: 1/3, 1/8, 1/6
learn.explanation: Same numerator (1). Smallest denominator = greatest fraction. 3 < 6 < 8 → 1/3 > 1/6 > 1/8.

learn.instruction: When fractions have DIFFERENT numerators AND denominators, use the 1/2 benchmark to sort them into groups first. Group A: less than 1/2. Group B: equal to 1/2. Group C: greater than 1/2. Then compare within each group. Example: Order 1/8, 5/6, 4/8. → 1/8 < 1/2 (Group A). 4/8 = 1/2 (Group B). 5/6 > 1/2 (Group C). → Least to greatest: 1/8, 4/8, 5/6.
learn.question: Sort into groups: 7/8, 2/6, 3/6. Which is less than 1/2? Equal to 1/2? Greater than 1/2?
learn.explanation: 2/6: 2 < 3 (half of 6) → less than 1/2. 3/6: 3 = 3 → equal to 1/2. 7/8: 7 > 4 (half of 8) → greater than 1/2.

try.question: Order from least to greatest: 6/8, 3/8, 1/8
try.hint: Same denominator. Just order the numerators.

try.question: Order from greatest to least: 2/4, 2/8, 2/3
try.hint: Same numerator. Smaller denominator = bigger fraction.

try.question: Order from least to greatest: 5/6, 1/8, 4/8
try.hint: Use the 1/2 benchmark to group them first.

practice_r1.question: Which list is in order from least to greatest?
Which fraction is the smallest: 4/8, 7/8, or 1/8?

practice_r2.question: Three jars are the same size. Jar A is 3/4 full. Jar B is 3/8 full. Jar C is 3/6 full. Order the jars from most full to least full.
practice_r2.answer: Jar A (3/4), Jar C (3/6), Jar B (3/8)

practice_r2.question: Which fraction belongs BETWEEN 2/8 and 6/8 on a number line?

practice_r3.question: Four students ran the same distance. Ava finished 7/8 of the run, Ben finished 2/8, Cal finished 5/8, and Dana finished 4/8. Order them from first place (most distance) to last place (least distance).
Put these fractions in order from least to greatest: 5/6, 2/8, 4/8. Explain which strategy you used and why.
Kai says to order 1/3, 1/6, 1/8 from least to greatest, you just order the denominators from least to greatest: 1/3, 1/6, 1/8. Is Kai correct? Explain.
practice_r3.answer: Kai is NOT correct. With the same numerator, a LARGER denominator means SMALLER pieces. So 1/8 < 1/6 < 1/3. The correct order from least to greatest is 1/8, 1/6, 1/3 — the denominators go from greatest to least.
